name: economic_wellbeing_perception
description: Measures how individuals evaluate their own and the country's economic situation, including intergenerational comparisons of living standards. Captures both personal and societal economic satisfaction as well as perceived upward or downward mobility across generations.

name: job_quality_and_labor_conditions
description: Measures the objective and subjective quality of current employment, including contract type, working hours, overtime compensation, social security coverage, income relative to peers, and satisfaction with advancement opportunities. Captures the multidimensional nature of decent work.

name: job_search_and_employment_precarity
description: Measures the experience of unemployment and job insecurity, including active job-seeking behavior, perceived probability of finding or losing work, duration of unemployment, and willingness to accept unfavorable job conditions. Reflects labor market vulnerability and precariousness.

name: labor_union_attitudes
description: Measures respondents' normative and evaluative orientations toward labor unions, including perceived necessity and overall opinion of unions' role. Captures the latent dimension of collective labor representation legitimacy.

name: human_capital_and_employment_preparation
description: Measures the relationship between education, training, and labor market readiness, including highest education attained, perceived educational adequacy for one's job, participation in professional development, and perceived utility of that training. Captures the latent construct of human capital investment and its labor market relevance.
